prompt: Name one commonly-praised idea, trend, or practice in modern knowledge work that you think is overrated, and explain why.
response: I think one idea that is often overrated is the notion of "busy work." Many people praise the idea of being constantly busy and engaged in various tasks or projects, equating busyness with productivity and success. However, this attitude can lead to a culture of burnout, where people feel pressured to take on more and more tasks, even if they're not essential or impactful, just to appear busy and valuable. In truth, focusing on impactful, meaningful work, even if it means having more downtime, is often more productive and sustainable in the long run.
